ref: Everyone in town came out to meet Jesus. When they saw him, they begged him to leave their part of the country.
text: Mɔ kulo nɩn asʋ menian'n-mɔ kʋalaa tɩlɩ ɛjɔlɛ sɔ'n, bɛ kɔ kpʋ Zozi. Mɔ bɛ nwunlin yɩ́'n, bɛ kɔ sɛlɛ yɩ́ kɛ ɔ fi bɛ́ mân nɩn anun ɔ jásʋ.
translated: A man went to the farm to work, and he met Zozi there. He asked her to help him weed the farm, because he was tired.

ref: You are a worthless servant, and you will be thrown out into the dark where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.” The Final Judgment
text: Nán akʋa mgban ɛhɩ, bɛ jú yɩ́ bɛ tʋ́ awosin nɩn anun ɛlɔ. Ɛbɛlɛ yɛ̂ ɔ 'sun ɛsɛ ɔ 'kʋan yɩ́ je nɩn ɔ.’ Kulo menian'n-mɔ kʋalaa ndɛɛ ebua'n
translated: The man went to the farm, he saw a woman who was weeding. He said to her, "What are you doing?" She replied, "I am weeding the farm."

ref: “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat.
text: “Menian ɛhɩ-mɔ anwʋn yɔ mɩ́n koun. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ bɛ nʋn mɩ́n wɔ ɛwa yɩ́ cɩan nsan ahɩ, bɛ nnyan man like fɩ́ɩ́ bɛ nni man.
translated: The woman who is going to be married is beautiful. She is loved by all the men in the village because she is kind and generous.

ref: What could you give to get back your soul?
text: Nzu like dɩɛ bɔbɔ yɛ̂ kulo sʋanlan kʋala kɛ ɔ fa kaci yɩ́ ngʋan nɩn ɔ?
translated: Why does the dog bark at the stranger who is passing by?

ref: Jesus and his disciples got up and went with the man.
text: Bian'n hanlɩn sɔ a, Zozi nʋn yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɔ jasʋ bɛ kɔ si yɩ́ sʋ.
translated: The old woman said, "Zozi, you are the one who will take care of my children when I am gone."

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Mɔ belemgbin Elɔdɩ nwunlin kɛ ngɛlɛfʋɛ'n-mɔ a laka yɩ́'n, ɔ fɛlɩ ɛya kpa. Ɔ tɩtɩlɩ cɩan'n mɔ ngɛlɛfʋɛ'n-mɔ nwunlin nzalama'n dede a, anɩn ɔ'a nwun kɛ batʋnman'n ka kaan di afʋɛ nnyuan. Yɩ́ ti, ɔ kɔ sʋan yɩ́ nzalafʋ'n-mɔ Betelehɛmʋn nʋn kulo'n-mɔ kʋalaa mɔ bɛ mantan mantan yɩ́'n-mɔ asʋ kɛ bɛ hɔ́ hún mmatʋnman mmelenzua mɔ b'a li afʋɛ nnyuan'n, ɔ nʋn bɛ́ mɔ bɛ lili man afʋɛ nnyuan'n kʋalaatin.
translated: The Lord said to Moses, "I have seen the affliction of my people who are in Egypt, and I have heard their cry because of their taskmasters. I know their sufferings, and I have come down to deliver them from the hand of the Egyptians and to bring them up out of that land to a good and spacious land, a land flowing with milk and honey, to the place of the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Amorites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites. Now therefore, behold, the cry of the Israelites has come to me, and I have seen the oppression with which the Egyptians oppress them. Come, I will send you to Pharaoh that you may bring my people, the Israelites, out of Egypt."

ref: You're nothing but show-offs! First, take the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye.
text: Ɛ bɔ wɔ́ nwʋn apoo, di mʋa yɩ tabua ebue'n m'ɔ la wɔ́ nyɩn nɩn asʋ'n, nán a nwun asɩ a yɩ tabua munmuin kaan'n m'ɔ la wɔ́ manngʋn nɩn anyɩn nɩn asʋ'n.
translated: When a person is born, they are given a name, and their lineage is traced back to their ancestors. This name and lineage are passed down through generations, connecting the living to their past.

ref: Jesus then told them this story: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a farmer scattered good seed in a field.
text: Ɛhɩ anzin, Zozi kɔ bu anyɩnndala kʋn biekun kɔ hele bɛ́. Yɩ́ nwan: “Ɛ 'nwun like mɔ Anwunno Belemgbin Mân'n soman yɩ́'n: Ɔ soman sʋanlan kʋn mɔ ɔ'a gua *bele yɩ́ ebo nɩn asʋ ɔ.
translated: Eh, Anzin, Zozi said that the Anyin people are not like the people of Anwunno Belemgbin Man's place: they don't have a place where they can go and get *bele yɩ́ ebo nɩn asʋ ɔ.

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
text: Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ sʋanlan m'ɔ le bie'n, bɛ 'man yɩ́ dɔʋn biekun. Nan sʋanlan kʋsʋ m'ɔ le man fɩ́ɩ́'n, bɛ 'tʋa yɩ́ bɛ de kaan'n m'ɔ le'n.
translated: He who is wise will not be deceived by appearances, but he who is foolish will be easily deceived. The wise man will see through the deception, but the foolish man will be caught in it.

text: Ɛmɔ hɔ́ ndɛ-ndɛ bɛ hán héle yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kɛ ɔ fi ewue nun ɔ'a tinnge. Sian dɩɛ, ɔ lɛ kɔ a nwʋnndɛ ɛmɔ Galile mân nɩn anun. Ɛlɔ yɛ̂ ɛmɔ 'nwun yɩ́ ɔ. Ngalɩɛ'n mɔ mɩ́n nwan mɩn 'kan mɩn kele ɛmɔ nɩn anɩn.”
translated: He went to the city, and there he found a man who was possessed by an unclean spirit. And he said to him, "Come out of him!" text: Yɩ́ sɔ ɛhɩ, anɩn bɛ tɩ man menian nnyuan kʋ́n, nan b'a yɔ sʋanlan munmuan kʋn. Like mɔ Nyanmɩan kɔ han kɔ bɔ nun'n, kulo sʋanlan n'ɔ tɩ nun.”
translated: Like a person who has a lot of things to do, but is only able to do a few, so it is with the world. God created everything, but we only see a small part of it.

ref: When Jesus and his three disciples came back down, they saw a large crowd around the other disciples. The teachers of the Law of Moses were arguing with them.
text: Zozi nʋn Pɩɛlɩ nʋn Zʋan nʋn Zʋakɩ fi bʋka nɩn asʋ bɛ kɔ ju. Mɔ bɛ walɩ dede mɔ bɛ ka kaan bɛ ju Zozi menian ɛhɩnlɩn-mɔ anwʋn ɛbɛlɛ'n, bɛ kɔ nwun meninsʋnman bɛ́ nwʋn ɛbɛlɛ. Mala nɩn asʋ mgbain'n-mɔ wɔ bɛ́ nun. Bɛ kɔ fa akpʋlʋwa esue bɛ kɔ tʋa Zozi menian'n-mɔ.
translated: Zozi, Pɛlɩ, Zʋan, and Zʋakɩ went to look for firewood. They found a lot of firewood and brought it home. Zozi's mother was happy to see them and gave them food to eat. They all ate and were satisfied. Then they went to play.

ref: But the guests did not pay any attention. Some of them left for their farms, and some went to their places of business.
text: “Bɛ hɔlɩ fɛlɛlɩ bɛ́ a, menian sɔ'n-mɔ ngɔ kpinlin belemgbin anwʋn. Ɛhɩ jasʋ a, ɔ lɛ kɔ yɩ́ ebolo, ɛhɩ jasʋ a, ɔ lɛ kɔ a tɔnɩn yɩ́ ninnge.
translated: When a person is in trouble, they should seek help from their elders. If they are sick, they should go to the doctor, if they are hungry, they should go to the market.

ref: A teacher of the Law of Moses came up to him and said, “Teacher, I'll go anywhere with you!”
text: Mala nɩn asʋ kpain'n kʋn kpunngelɩ yɩ́ nwʋn ɛbɛlɛ a, yɩ́ nwan: “Kpain, lɩka biala mɔ ɛ 'kɔ'n, mɩn 'kɔ bie.”
translated: Mala said to his friend, "Friend, if you see a white bird, don't shoot it, let it go."

ref: On the day of judgment the people of Nineveh will stand there with you and condemn you. They turned to God when Jonah preached, and yet here is something far greater than Jonah.
text: Ndɛɛ ebua cɩan nɩn anun, Ninninvʋ amma'n-mɔ 'bu ajulisʋ menian'n-mɔ fʋɔ. Ɔ sanlɩn kɛ mɔ Ninninvʋ kulo nɩn asʋ amma'n-mɔ tɩlɩ Nyanmɩan Anʋan Ɛjɔlɛ'n mɔ Zonasɩ hanlɩn'n, bɛ nunlin bɛ́ nwʋn bɛ kacili bɛ́ abalabɔ'n. Nan sʋanlan kʋn wɔ ɛwa, ɔ tɩ kpili tala Zonasɩ.
translated: The people of Anyin have been living in peace and harmony. They have a strong belief in their ancestors and their traditions. They respect their elders and value their wisdom. They are known for their hospitality and generosity. They are a hardworking and resilient people who have overcome many challenges. They are proud of their culture and heritage.

ref: After Jesus left the crowd and went inside, his disciples came to him and said, “Explain to us the story about the weeds in the wheat field.”
text: Mɔ Zozi hanlɩn sɔ yuelɩ'n, ɔ gualɩ meninsʋnman nɩn atɩn yɛ̂ ɔ hɔlɩ awulo ɔ. Yɩ́ menian'n-mɔ kpunngelɩ yɩ́ a, bɛ́ nwan: “Tu ndile ɛtɛ'n m'ɔ fifi ebo nɩn asʋ nɩn anwʋn anyɩnndala nɩn abʋ kele yɛ́.”
translated: Zozi said to his younger brother, "I have seen a beautiful woman, and I want to marry her. Go and tell her father that I want to marry his daughter."

ref: Don't be ashamed of me and my message among these unfaithful and sinful people! If you are, the Son of Man will be ashamed of you when he comes in the glory of his Father with the holy angels.
text: Ajulisʋ ɛtɛfʋɛ menian ɛhɩ-mɔ mɔ bɛ nzulo man Nyanmɩan'n, bɛ́ afian, wɔ́ mɔ ɛ kɔ sa mɩ́n nʋn mɩ́n ɛjɔlɛ nɩn anwʋn nyian'n, *Mân Baa'n kʋsʋ, mɛlɛ m'ɔ nʋn yɩ́ nyanmɩansʋ anwʋntɩɛ mmɔfʋɛ'n-mɔ 'ba sa bɛ ba Nyanmɩan anunminnyanmʋn nɩn anun'n, wɔ́ nwʋn 'ba yɔ yɩ́ nyian.”
translated: The ancestors of the Nyanmɩan people, who are the elders of our land, say that we should not forget the stories of our ancestors, for they are the foundation of our culture and identity. They say, "Let us remember the stories of our ancestors, for they are the roots of our being. They are the ones who guide us and protect us."
